hook: You've rephrased the same prompt 4 times and the AI still sounds like a LinkedIn post from 2019.
caption:
You've rephrased the same prompt 4 times and the AI still sounds like a LinkedIn post from 2019.

Here's what's actually happening.

AI doesn't know if you're the person who needs the full logical argument before you'll believe anything — or the person who just wants the answer, skip the explanation, don't even say 'certainly.'

It doesn't know you hate bullet points. Or that you think in analogies. Or that 'be concise' to you means two sentences, not two paragraphs.

So it guesses. And it guesses toward the average. Which is why everything sounds the same.

The fix isn't a better prompt. The fix is a profile — a persistent set of instructions that travels with you, so the AI already knows how your brain works before you type a single word.

That's what bootfile.ai builds for you.

You answer a few questions about how you think, how you communicate, what you're actually trying to do — and it generates a custom instruction profile you can drop into any AI tool.

First response. Right tone. No fourth draft.

Link in bio to build yours.
